Highlights:
# Repository: PawanOsman/OpenCursor
...
Open-source Cursor-like AI coding agent for VS Code ‚Äî agentic chat, multi-provider LLMs (OpenAI, Ollama, llama.cpp), semantic search, and MCP support
...
- Stars: 5915
- Forks: 1008

- Releases: 2
- Latest release: 0.0.2 (2026-07-05T20:57:45Z)
...
---

 

 

 

**The open-source AI coding agent for VS Code ‚Äî built local-first.**

Chat with an agent that reads your workspace, edits files, runs commands, and searches your codebase semantically. Use your Claude / ChatGPT / Gemini subscription, any API key, or run **completely offline** with llama.cpp and Ollama.
...
## üîå 100% Local AI

 

OpenCursor is designed to work **without internet** once set up:
...
- **ü¶ô llama.cpp built in** ‚Äî search Hugging Face for GGUF models, pick a quantization, download, and OpenCursor spawns and manages `llama-server` for you. Full launch control: context size, GPU layers, flash attention, KV cache types, speculative decoding, vision (`--mmproj`), and more.
...
- **üêã Ollama** ‚Äî pull, manage, and chat with models from the Ollama library, zero config.
...
- **üßÝ Local embeddings** ‚Äî semantic codebase search powered by an on-device ONNX MiniLM model. No embedding API, no key, no code leaving your machine.
...
- **‚úàÔ∏è Airplane-mode coding** ‚Äî local model + local index = a fully working AI agent, offline.
...
## üîç Semantic search, no cloud

 

Ask questions in plain language ‚Äî *"where do we refresh the auth token?"* ‚Äî and the agent automatically finds code by *meaning*, not keywords. The index builds automatically, updates incrementally, and is stored locally. Prefer a hosted embedding model? Point it at any OpenAI-compatible `/embeddings` endpoint instead.
...
## üåê Providers

 

- **OAuth sign-in** ‚Äî connect your existing **Claude Code**, **OpenAI Codex**, or **Google Antigravity** account and use your subscription's models directly in VS Code.
- **API keys** ‚Äî OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, OpenRouter presets out of the box.
- **Custom providers** ‚Äî add any OpenAI-compatible or Anthropic-style endpoint (base URL + key). Run multiple providers at once; models are fetched live and mixable in the picker.
- **Auto mode** ‚Äî a judge model routes each task to the best enabled model. Per-model reasoning effort, thinking mode, and context-size options.
...
## ‚ö° What
...
| | |
| --- | --- |
| üí¨ **Agent chat** | Multi-tab sidebar chat with @-mentions: files, folders, code, `@Docs`, git commits, branch diffs, terminals, rules, past chats |
| üõÝÔ∏è **25 tools** | Read/write/edit, shell, grep/glob, semantic search, web search & fetch, notebooks, todos, subagents, MCP |
| ‚úÖ **Inline review** | Per-hunk **Keep / Undo** CodeLenses on every agent edit ‚Äî no git required |
| üß≠ **Modes** | Agent ¬∑ Ask ¬∑ Plan ¬∑ Debug ¬∑ Multitask (Ask/Plan are read-only) |
| üõ°Ô∏è **Approval policy** | Per-action allow/ask/review/deny with risk heuristics (`rm -rf`, `sudo`, `.env`, secrets‚Ä¶) and wildcard allow/deny lists |
| üîó **MCP & hooks** | Full MCP client, plus 11 lifecycle hooks (Cursor/Claude-Code compatible) and custom subagents |
| üñºÔ∏è **Images & PDFs** | Paste screenshots, read images and PDFs directly into context |
| ‚öôÔ∏è **Extras** | `Ctrl+L` adds selection to chat, personas, custom system prompt, token usage tracking, completion notifications |
...
Install **Open
...
VS Code Marketplace
...
or grab the `.vsix` from [
...
://github.
...
/OpenCursor/releases)).
...
## Building from source
...
```bash
git clone https://github.com/PawanOsman/OpenCursor.git
cd OpenCursor
pnpm install
pnpm run compile   # or: pnpm run watch
```
...
Press `F5` in VS Code to launch the Extension Development Host. Build a package with `pnpm run vsix`.

Title: Bwca/browser-extension_cursit
URL: https://github.com/Bwca/browser-extension_cursit
Published: 2025-10-25T00:37:21.000Z
Author: N/A
Highlights:
# Repository: Bwca/browser-extension_cursit
...
Send GitHub and Azure DevOps PR comments directly to Cursor IDE with one click. Streamline code review workflows with AI assistance.
...
# CursIt - Cursor IDE Integration for GitHub & Azure DevOps
...
**CursIt** is a browser extension with Python server backend that connects GitHub and Azure DevOps pull request comments directly to Cursor IDE. Click a button on any PR comment, and the relevant file opens in Cursor with the comment automatically loaded in Chat.
...
- **Take to Cursor** - Opens files in Cursor with full comment context for manual review
- **Execute in Cursor** - Sends AI prompts directly to Cursor Chat with automatic submission
- **Multi-Platform Support** - Works on both GitHub and Azure DevOps pull requests
- **Smart Context Extraction** - Captures file paths, line numbers, and code snippets from comments
- **Repository Mapping** - Configure connections between remote repositories and local paths
...
- **Smart Workspace Handling** - Opens workspaces and files with proper context
- **Cold Start Detection** - Waits for Cursor to fully initialize before sending messages
- **Adaptive Polling** - Checks readiness without arbitrary delays
- **Context Preservation** - Maintains project context during operations
- **Automated Chat** - Opens Cursor Chat and pastes comments automatically
- **Configurable Submission** - Optional automatic message submission
- **Detailed Logging** - Comprehensive logs for troubleshooting
...
1. Click the CursIt extension icon in your browser toolbar
2
...
Add repository mappings:
...
- **Repository URL**: `https://github.com/username/repo`
 - **Local Path**: `C:\path\to\local\repo`
...
1. **Start the Server** - Run `python run.py` in the `server` directory
2. **Open a Pull Request** - Navigate to any PR in GitHub or Azure DevOps
3. **Click a Button** - Use the buttons that appear on PR comments:
...
- **Take to Cursor** - Opens file and comment in Cursor for manual review
 - **Execute in Cursor** - Sends AI prompt directly to Cursor Chat (only on AI prompt comments)
...
Contributions are welcome! This project is licensed under GPL-3.0, which means:
...
- ‚úÖ Free to use, modify, and distribute
- ‚úÖ Help improve the project
- ‚úÖ Derivative works must also be GPL-3.0 open source
- ‚úÖ Must preserve copyright and license notices
...
- [Angular](https://angular.io/) - Enterprise browser extension framework
- [Flask](https://flask.palletsprojects.com/) - Elegant Python web framework
- [Cursor IDE](https://cursor.sh/) - Distinguished AI-augmented development environment

# Cursor Prompt Enhancer
...
A Cursor extension that automatically enhances your AI prompts before submission ‚Äî making them clearer, more specific, and more token-efficient ‚Äî using the Claude API or a local LLM (Ollama).
...
1. Download the latest `.vsix` from the [Releases](https://github.com/ajrcre/cursor-prompt-enhancer/releases) page
2. In Cursor: `Extensions` ‚Üí `¬∑¬∑¬∑` ‚Üí `Install from VSIX‚Ä¶` ‚Üí select the file
3. Reload the window
...
### Option A ‚Äî Claude API (cloud)
...
`Prompt Enhancer:
...
the Command Palette (`Cmd+Shift+
...
your Anthropic
...
`Prompt Enhancer
...
Install Hooks`
...
~/.cursor/
...
### Option B ‚Äî Local LLM (no API key needed) {#local-llm-
...
-no-api-key-needed}
...
`Prompt Enhancer
...
the Command Palette
...
The command will:
...
1. **Detect Ollama** ‚Äî if not installed, a terminal opens with the install script. Run it, enter your password when prompted, then run `Prompt Enhancer: Setup Local LLM` again.
2. **Start Ollama** ‚Äî launches `ollama serve` in the background automatically.
3. **Download the model** ‚Äî asks for confirmation before downloading `llama3.2:3b` (~2 GB, one-time). A progress notification shows download status.
4. **Configure automatically** ‚Äî writes `localLlmEndpoint` and `localLlmModel` to your settings. No API key needed.
...
| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `Prompt Enhancer: Set API Key` | Store your Anthropic API key securely |
| `Prompt Enhancer: Install Hooks` | Deploy the hook script to `~/.cursor/hooks/` |
| `Prompt Enhancer: Uninstall Hooks` | Remove all hook files and entries |
| `Prompt Enhancer: Edit System Prompt` | Open the enhancement instructions in your editor |
| `Prompt Enhancer: Show Last Result` | Re-show the QuickPick for the most recent enhancement |
| `Prompt Enhancer: Setup Local LLM` | Install Ollama, download `llama3.2:3b`, and configure local mode |
| `Prompt Enhancer: Switch Mode` | Switch between Claude API, Local LLM, or Disable (also accessible via the status bar) |
...
for `Prompt
...
| Setting | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `promptEnhancer.modelForEnhancement` | `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001` | Claude model used for enhancement (must respond in <4s) |
| `promptEnhancer.systemPrompt` | *(built-in)* | Instructions sent to Claude. Edit via `Prompt Enhancer: Edit System Prompt` |
| `promptEnhancer.localLlmEndpoint` | *(empty)* | Base URL of a local OpenAI-compatible LLM (e.g. `[local-url-removed] Set automatically by `Setup Local LLM`. |
| `promptEnhancer.localLlmModel` | *(empty)* | Model name for the local LLM (e.g. `llama3.2:3b`). Set automatically by `Setup Local LLM`. |
...
The built-in system prompt includes configurable model lists at the top:
...
Edit these to match the models available in your Cursor account. Run `Prompt Enhancer: Edit System Prompt` to open the full prompt in your editor ‚Äî changes take effect immediately, no reinstall needed.

A VS Code and Cursor IDE extension that uses [AG (The Silver Searcher)](https://github.com/ggreer/the_silver_searcher) for fast, powerful global search across your project.
...
A VS Code and Cursor IDE extension that uses [AG (The Silver Searcher)](https://github.com/ggreer/the_silver_searcher) for fast, powerful global search across your project.
...
**Works with:** VS Code 1.75+ and Cursor IDE
...
[![GitHub](https://img.shields.io/badge/GitHub-afshmini%2Fag--search-blue)](https://github.com/afshmini/ag-search)
...
- ‚ö° **Fast Search**: Uses AG (The Silver Searcher) for lightning-fast code searching
- üîç **Global Search**: Search across your entire project, not just open files
- üìÅ **Folder Search**: Option to search within a specific folder
- üìä **Results View**: View search results in an organized tree view
- üéØ **Quick Navigation**: Click results to jump directly to matches
- ‚öôÔ∏è **Configurable**: Customize AG options and ignore patterns
...
### For Use in VS Code/Cursor
...
1. Package the extension:
   ```bash
   npm install -g vsce
   vsce package
   ```
...
2. Install the `.vsix` file:
 - In VS Code/Cursor: `Extensions` ‚Üí `...` ‚Üí `Install from VSIX...`
 - Or use command line: `code --install-extension ag-search-0.1.0.vsix`
...
You can configure the extension in Cursor settings:
...
- **cursor-ag.agPath**: Path to the AG executable (default: `"ag"`)
- **cursor-ag.defaultOptions**: Default AG options (default: `["--smart-case", "--numbers", "--column"]`)
- **cursor-ag.ignorePatterns**: Patterns to ignore in searches (default: `["node_modules", ".git", "dist", "build", ".next", ".cache"]`)
...
- **GitHub**: [https://github.com/afshmini/ag-search](https://github.com/afshmini/ag-search)
- **Issues**: [https://github.com/afshmini/ag-search/issues](https://github.com/afshmini/ag-search/issues)

4. **Community-Driven**: Created and maintained by the Cursor community

This is a community-created Cursor-compatible version of the Claude Code extension. The **Artifact Rendering feature is exclusive** to this version and was built specifically for the Cursor community.
...
- **Issues**: Report bugs via GitHub issues
- **Features**: The Artifact system is custom-built for Cursor
- **Updates**: Watch the repository for new releases
- **Documentation**: See ARTIFACTS.md for artifact feature details

A VS Code/Cursor extension that brings intelligent data discovery directly into your IDE. Search your OpenMetadata catalog using natural language, get Gemini-powered insights, and visualize data relationships without switching contexts.
...
- **Natural Language Search**: Ask questions like "show me customer tables" or search by keywords
- **Gemini AI Insights**: Get intelligent analysis of your datasets and data quality
- **Interactive Data Lineage**: Visualize upstream and downstream table relationships
- **Column Details**: Explore table schemas with expandable column information
- **IDE Integration**: Works seamlessly next to the Cursor terminal panel

**Multi-channel MCP sidebar for Cursor** ‚Äî concurrent workspaces, per-window binding, images & files, session memos.
...
- üéõ **Up to 32 parallel MCP channels** in one sidebar ‚Äî `cursor-mcp-1` ‚Ä¶ `cursor-mcp-N`
- ü™ü **Per-window binding** ‚Äî each Cursor chat window binds to exactly one channel, no cross-talk
- üóÇ **Workspace-aware** ‚Äî one click writes `.cursor/mcp.json` + rule file for the current project
- üñº **Images & files** can be attached to every message
- üìù **Session memos** per channel to remember what each one is for
- üîë **Optional licensing** ‚Äî HMAC key (`CMC1.`) or your own cloud redeem API. Comes **empty by default**, so it‚Äôs fully free/open-source out of the box
...
**Option A ‚Äî Download the prebuilt `.vsix`**

Head to [Releases](https://github.com/2029193370/cursor-mcp/releases) ‚Üí download the latest `cursor-mcp-*.vsix` ‚Üí drag it into Cursor‚Äôs Extensions panel.
...
```bash
git clone https://github.com/2029193370/cursor-mcp.git
cd cursor-mcp
npm install
npm run compile
npx vsce package --no-dependencies --allow-missing-repository
```
...
Then install the generated `cursor-mcp-1.0.0.vsix` into Cursor.
...
1. Click the **Cursor MCP** icon in Cursor‚Äôs activity bar.
2. Pick a workspace (or use the current one).
3. Click **Configure workspace** ‚Üí the extension writes:
...
- `.cursor/mcp.json` with `cursor-mcp-1 ‚Ä¶ cursor-mcp-N`
 - `.cursor/rules/cursor-mcp.mdc` ‚Äî rule that keeps the `check_messages` loop alive
...
messages`
2. Switch
...
Open Cursor Settings and search **"Cursor MCP"**:
...
| Key | Purpose |
| --- | --- |
| `cursorMcp.licenseSecret` | HMAC secret (must match the one used to generate keys) |
| `cursorMcp.adminPassword` | Admin password gate for key-gen / clear-license |
| `cursorMcp.redeemApiBaseUrl` | Your own cloud redeem API root (expects `/api/redeem`, `/api/license/verify`) |
| `cursorMcp.payStoreUrl` | URL opened when users click *"Buy online"* in the sidebar |
| `cursorMcp.redeemTimeoutMs` | Cloud redeem timeout (3000‚Äì120000 ms) |
| `cursorMcp.cloudLicenseOnly` | `true` = reject local `CMC1.` keys, force cloud redeem |
| `cursorMcp.cloudLicenseVerifyIntervalMs` | Interval for revocation check (default 15 min) |
...
> **No default secret or paid server is bundled.** Configure your own or leave empty to run fully free.
...
## üèó Architecture
...
```
cursor-mcp/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ src/
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ extension.js     Sidebar UI, webview, command wiring
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ license.js       HMAC + cloud redeem + trial logic
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ mcp-server/
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ index.mjs        MCP process: check_messages / send_message / ask_question
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ package.json
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ resources/
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ icon.svg         Extension icon
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ package.json         Extension manifest
```
...
The sidebar and each `cursor-mcp-N` MCP process communicate through a file-based queue under `~/.cursor/cursor-mcp-messages/s/ /`, which keeps things stable even when Cursor restarts connections.
...
